Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement And Dismissal Of X-Complaints
Off Calendar Nov-18-2004 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
William C. Howison, Esq., Baraban & Teske (626) 440-9882 for Cross Complainant and Moving Party turan and Royal Taxi Co.
Laura Sagmeister-Flynn, Esq., Drath Clifford Murphy & Hagen LLP (510) 287-4050 for Defendant Allied Shuttle
RULING:
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. The Court's tentative ruling (Motion Continued to 12/03/04 so that settling parties may provide non-settling parties with a copy of settlement agreement) is withdrawn upon declaration of Attorney Howison, under penalty of perjury, that the amended settlement agreement of 10/26/04 (already supplied to all parties) contains all the terms and conditions of its settlement with Plaintiff Spaulding. Based on that representation, and upon full consideration of all papers in support of and in opposition to the pending matter, the Court finds that all of the Tech-Bilt factors have been satisfied; the Court finds that the settlement was made in good faith. The motion is therefoe GRANTED. The Court further finds the reasonable contingent set-off value of the sliding scale settlement, which may be used in connection with ongoing settlement negotiations, is $35,000.00. Prevailing party to submit form of order. (301/JLW/DS) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
